title: Introduction
description: NOC 64101 – Sales and account representatives - wholesale trade | GTR Immigration In Canada's robust B2B economy, NOC 64101 (Sales and account representatives - wholesale trade) are the essential link between manufacturers and industrial, commercial, and professional clients. Unlike technical sales, these representatives focus on non-technical goods such as food, textiles, and consumer products. As a TEER 4 occupation, this role offers a highly lucrative and stable entry point into the Canadian labor market. For immigration, it is a top-performing category for Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs) and provides a direct path to senior account management and corporate leadership.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category6 – Sales and service occupationsTEER Level4Major Group64 – Sales and service representatives and other customer and personal services occupationsSub-Major Group641 – Sales and account representativesMinor Group6410 – Sales and account representativesNOC Code64101

duties: Promote sales to existing clients and identify and solicit new potential clients.
Provide customers with specialized information on product features, prices, and availability.
Negotiate prices, credit terms, and transportation arrangements and prepare sales contracts.
Consult with clients after sale or signed contracts to resolve problems and provide ongoing support.
Review and adapt information regarding product innovations and competitors' products and prices.
May supervise the activities of other sales representatives.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
A university degree or college diploma in business or a related field is often preferred.
Several years of experience as a retail salesperson or in a related service occupation are usually required.
A valid driver's license and a good driving record are typically necessary for client visits.
job_outlook: The outlook for this occupation is Favourable. According to the Canadian Occupational Projection System (COPS), over 30,000 new job openings are expected through 2031. Growth is driven by the expansion of the "Direct-to-Business" e-commerce sector and the modernization of wholesale distribution networks.
labour_market_demand: The demand for wholesale sales representatives is currently Very Good, particularly in major distribution hubs like Mississauga (Ontario), Surrey (British Columbia), and Laval (Quebec). As Canadian businesses expand their supply chains to combat global disruptions, the need for account representatives who can secure new vendors and maintain high-volume corporate accounts is at an all-time high.
High Earning Potential: Base salaries are frequently supplemented by uncapped commission structures and performance bonuses.
Corporate Mobility: Rapid progression to Corporate Sales Manager (NOC 60010) or Purchasing Manager (NOC 60020).
Strategic Networking: Work directly with the leadership of major Canadian retail and industrial firms.
Stable Sector: Wholesale trade is a cornerstone of the Canadian economy, ensuring long-term job security.
pathways: Provincial Nominee Program (PNP): Entry-level and semi-skilled streams in Alberta, BC, and Saskatchewan.
Atlantic Immigration Program (AIP): A high-conversion pathway for representatives with job offers in the Atlantic provinces.
CEC (Canadian Experience Class): Accessible for those who have completed 12 months of work in Canada on a valid permit.
Rural and Northern Immigration Pilot (RNIP): Addressing trade needs in participating small-town communities.
